adversarial.notes: Independently re-derived (not trusted from generator evidence, Mode B's own claims, or this evaluator's own prior Loop-1 scratch-schema probes) by exercising the REAL, implemented schema/engineering_finding.schema.json directly with fresh hand-built instances reproducing the FULL three-round Loop-1 kill-shot battery (revisions 1, 2, and 3) against the shipped artifact, not scratch prototypes: (1) jsonschema.Draft202012Validator.check_schema confirms the shipped schema is itself valid Draft 2020-12. (2) ROUND-1 BATTERY: each of the five minLength:1 required scalar fields (id, severity, impact, owner, closure_criteria), independently set to '' on an otherwise-complete instance -- all 5 correctly REJECTED ('should be non-empty'); an evidence item path '/etc/passwd' and, separately, 'evidence/e/1.txt' (missing the '.agent/' prefix) -- both correctly REJECTED, while a conforming '.agent/evidence/...' path validates; a bogus maturity value 'Battle-Tested' -- correctly REJECTED, and each of the ten real protocol-Sec-4 level names individually validates. (3) ROUND-2 BATTERY: a nested path containing the '.agent/evidence/' substring but not as a true prefix ('foo/.agent/evidence/bar.txt') -- correctly REJECTED, confirming the pattern's leading '^' anchor is genuinely load-bearing in the shipped file, not merely in a scratch copy. (4) ROUND-3 BATTERY: status='reopened' and status='closed' -- both correctly REJECTED by the enum; status='open' with no closure_evidence, and status='resolved' with complete closure_evidence, both correctly validate; a top-level evidence:[] (empty array) -- correctly REJECTED; a resolved instance with closure_evidence:[] (empty array, distinct from both the missing-key case and the closure_evidence:[{}] structurally-empty-item case) -- correctly REJECTED; closure_evidence:[{}] -- correctly REJECTED ('path' is a required property); resolved with no closure_evidence key at all -- correctly REJECTED. (5) CARRIED-FORWARD MECHANISMS re-probed directly against the shipped file: each of the 8 required top-level keys, independently omitted, correctly REJECTED (8/8); a bogus confidence value 'Definitely' correctly REJECTED, all three real values (Confirmed/Probable/Suspected) validate; an evidence item missing 'revision', and separately missing 'path', both correctly REJECTED. Every one of these approximately 40 direct probes matched its designed expectation exactly against the shipped, implemented schema -- the full three-round kill-shot lineage this evaluator authored across Loop 1 is confirmed genuinely closed in the real artifact, not merely in disposable scratch copies. All 5 shipped tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_*.json fixtures were independently validated directly against the shipped schema (independent of the contract's own glob/named-file verification logic): the 2 positive fixtures (open, resolved-with-complete-closure-evidence) both VALID; the 3 negative fixtures (resolved-without-closure-evidence, resolved-with-closure_evidence:[{}], missing-owner) all correctly REJECTED, each citing the expected distinct violation ('closure_evidence' required; 'path' required; 'owner' required). Independently re-confirmed NIZAM.json's two indexing surfaces (top-level 'schemas' array AND the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array) both name the new schema, and schema/README.md's frontmatter version (0.7.0, strictly increased over HEAD's 0.6.0) has a matching change_log entry and the '## Schemas' section names the new file -- all via fresh, hand-written python/grep, not the contract's own command bodies. Cross-checked .agent/evidence/039/diff.patch's sha256 against a live `git diff HEAD | sha256sum`: identical (30c060261737571d085c0bc5d95b911cdff3b76c29166863af9fbbbe722d455c), confirming the evidence bundle's diff artifact is the genuine, current tracked diff (NIZAM.json + schema/README.md, 8 insertions/2 deletions), not stale or fabricated.

observations: ALL 35 VERIFICATION ENTRIES RE-RUN LIVE FROM REPO ROOT (commands extracted programmatically from the live, approved .agent/contracts/039.json revision 3, not retyped): 35/35 PASS (EXIT:0), matching the evidence bundle's own claimed 35/35 and this evaluator's own independent execution. Zero mismatches between the live re-run and the checked-in .agent/evidence/039-verify-NN.txt files: every file's first line matches its contract command's first line verbatim, and every file's last line is a literal 'EXIT:0' (programmatically verified across all 35, not spot-checked).

THE FULL THREE-ROUND KILL-SHOT LINEAGE (this evaluator's own rev1/rev2/rev3 contract-review battery) WAS RE-EXERCISED DIRECTLY AGAINST THE SHIPPED, IMPLEMENTED SCHEMA, NOT MERELY THE CONTRACT'S OWN COMMAND BODIES: minLength empty-string rejection (5/5 fields), evidence-path prefix-pattern rejection (both the original bad-prefix probes and the nested-substring anchor probe), maturity exact-vocabulary enforcement (bogus rejected, 10/10 real levels validate), status-enum rejection ('reopened'/'closed' both rejected, including the specific combined kill-shot Mode A's round-2 finding names), top-level evidence:[] rejection, and resolved+closure_evidence:[] rejection (distinct from both the missing-key and closure_evidence:[{}] cases) -- every probe in this evaluator's entire Loop-1 history behaves exactly as designed against the real artifact. This is independent confirmation that Mode B's own claimed 're-probed against the shipped schema' battery is genuine, not merely asserted.

DUAL-SURFACE NIZAM.json INDEXING CONFIRMED DIRECTLY: 'schema/engineering_finding.schema.json' is present in BOTH the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array of the live NIZAM.json -- independently re-checked via fresh python, not the contract's own command bodies.

SCHEMA/README.MD INDEXING AND VERSION DISCIPLINE CONFIRMED DIRECTLY: the '## Schemas' table carries a new row for engineering_finding.schema.json; the frontmatter version is 0.7.0, a strict increase over HEAD's 0.6.0 (037's own prior bump); a matching change_log entry for version 0.7.0 is present, per the house 0.3.0/.../0.6.0 precedent this file already established.

DIFF.PATCH INTEGRITY: `sha256sum .agent/evidence/039/diff.patch` and a live `git diff HEAD | sha256sum` both produce 30c060261737571d085c0bc5d95b911cdff3b76c29166863af9fbbbe722d455c -- byte-identical, confirming the evidence bundle's diff artifact is genuine and current, not stale. The diff touches exactly NIZAM.json and schema/README.md (8 insertions, 2 deletions), matching contract scope.files_modify exactly; no other tracked file is touched. .agent/evidence/039/verification.txt independently reports the same 35/35 total this evaluator's own live re-run reproduced.

IMPLEMENTATION SCOPE CONFIRMED VIA LIVE git status: exactly the 2 authorized tracked modifications (NIZAM.json, schema/README.md) plus the 6 authorized new untracked files (schema/engineering_finding.schema.json and the 5 tools/fixtures/engineering_finding_*.json fixtures) are present, plus this feature's own evidence/.agent/qa artifacts and the pre-existing, tolerated v0.6.0-release-notes.md and .agent/contracts/040.json (feature 040 drafting -- confirmed untouched, never opened, by this QA pass). `git diff --quiet HEAD -- .agent/run_state.json` confirms run_state.json is byte-identical to HEAD, untouched by this pass or by the implementation.

FEATURE_LIST_005.JSON ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA INDEPENDENTLY RE-RUN, LITERAL TEXT, NOT THE CONTRACT'S PARAPHRASED MIRROR: all 4 embedded-command acceptance tests for feature 039 ('the schema is itself a valid JSON Schema and names the required finding fields...', 'a positive finding fixture validates AND a negative finding fixture...is rejected...', 'NIZAM.json indexes the new schema and stays C4-green...', 'compliance validator stays green: ...SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed') were extracted verbatim from .agent/feature_list_005.json and re-run directly: all 4 PASS (EXIT:0), including the LITERAL, hardcoded-count form of the validate.sh check ('SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed' exactly, not merely the contract's more general '[0-9]+ passed, 0 failed' regex) -- both forms currently agree since the check count has not changed since feature 038. The two prose-only criteria ('Severity, confidence, evidence, impact, owner, closure criteria supported', 'Positive and negative fixtures exist') are satisfied directly by the shipped schema's 8 required top-level keys and the 5 shipped fixtures (2 positive, 3 negative), independently confirmed via this QA pass's own adversarial instance probes and direct fixture validation. NO [AMENDED] RECONCILIATION FLAG IS NEEDED: feature_list_005.json's acceptance_tests array for feature 039 is unchanged from the version this evaluator reviewed across all three Loop-1 contract-review rounds, and the shipped implementation satisfies it without any reinterpretation, substitution, or narrowing.

bash tools/validate.sh: 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed', independently re-run and confirmed by this QA pass, matching both the evidence bundle's claim and every prior round's baseline (unchanged check count since feature 038).

PRE-EXISTING, NON-BLOCKING OBSERVATION CARRIED FORWARD FOR THE RECORD ONLY (per this evaluator's own .agent/qa/039-contract-review-rev3.json, unchanged since revision 0, not a new finding and not gating this verdict): schema_design's prose describes 'id' as 'unique within the audit run', a claim this single-finding-object schema cannot itself structurally enforce (a findings.json array-level schema, out of this feature's scope per its own non_goals, would be needed to express cross-record uniqueness). Recorded here again only for completeness, exactly as at contract-review time; not a QA defect since no acceptance test in this feature or feature_list_005.json requires it.

run_state.json, feature 039's status field in .agent/feature_list_005.json (still 'pending'), and every 040 surface were not touched, read-modified, or otherwise altered by this QA pass; feature 039's status flip and run_state.json update remain the orchestrator's own subsequent action, per the task's explicit instruction not to perform them here.

final_verdict.summary: PASS. All 35 contract (revision 3, final permitted round) verification commands independently re-run from repo root and all exited 0/PASS, matching the evidence bundle's own claimed 35/35 exactly, with zero first-line or exit-code mismatches across all 35 .agent/evidence/039-verify-NN.txt files. This evaluator independently re-exercised the shipped schema/engineering_finding.schema.json with its own fresh probe instances (not the contract's own command bodies), reproducing the ENTIRE three-round Loop-1 kill-shot battery this evaluator authored (minLength empty-string rejection on 5 fields; evidence-path prefix-pattern rejection including the nested-substring anchor probe; maturity exact-ten-level-vocabulary enforcement; status-enum rejection including the specific combined 'closed'-with-no-closure_evidence kill shot; top-level evidence:[] rejection; resolved+closure_evidence:[] rejection distinct from both the missing-key and closure_evidence:[{}] cases; the 8-required-key omission loop; the confidence enum; evidence-item missing-path/missing-revision rejection) -- every probe matched its designed expectation exactly against the real, shipped artifact, confirming the full kill-shot lineage is genuinely closed in the implementation, not merely in scratch prototypes. All 5 shipped fixtures were independently validated directly against the shipped schema (2 positive VALID, 3 negative REJECTED with the expected distinct violations). NIZAM.json's two indexing surfaces and schema/README.md's Schemas-table row, version bump (0.6.0 -> 0.7.0), and matching change_log entry were independently re-confirmed. The evidence bundle is genuine and current: all 35 flat 039-verify-NN.txt files carry the exact invocations with a literal EXIT:0 as their last line; .agent/evidence/039/diff.patch's sha256 is byte-identical to a live `git diff HEAD` (30c060261737571d085c0bc5d95b911cdff3b76c29166863af9fbbbe722d455c), confirming it is not stale; .agent/evidence/039/verification.txt's narrative matches this pass's own independent findings (35/35). All 6 acceptance criteria in .agent/feature_list_005.json for feature 039 (2 prose-only, 4 embedded-command, including the literal hardcoded-count validate.sh form) were independently re-run verbatim and all pass; no [AMENDED] reconciliation is needed since the acceptance criteria are unchanged from every Loop-1 review and the shipped implementation satisfies them without reinterpretation. `bash tools/validate.sh` reports 'SUMMARY: 11 passed, 0 failed'. Live `git status` confirms the implementation's tracked diff is confined to exactly NIZAM.json and schema/README.md, plus the 6 authorized new untracked files, with .agent/run_state.json, feature 039's own feature_list status, and every 040 surface confirmed untouched by this pass. One pre-existing, non-blocking observation (schema_design's 'id... unique within the audit run' prose claim, structurally outside a single-finding-object schema's own reach) is carried forward for the record only, unchanged since Loop-1, and does not gate this verdict. Feature 039 is approved for Loop-2 completion; status flip and run_state.json update are left to the orchestrator, per instruction.
